revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|12=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|12=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|12=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|12=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|12=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|12=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|12=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|12=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|12=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|12=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft|12=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=draft|12=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|12=draft|12=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft|12=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft|12=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=draft|12=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft|12=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|12=draft|12=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|12=draft|12=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|12=draft|12=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|12=draft|12=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=draft|12=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|12=draft|12=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|12=draft|12=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=draft|12=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}}

revision.comment: 10 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:住吉大伴神社 up to rev 74941999
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 住吉大伴神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]龍安寺住吉町1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |祭神 = [[天忍日命]]
[[道臣命]]
[[住吉三神]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[住吉造]]檜皮妻入 |別名 = |例祭 =10月16日 |神事 = }} '''住吉大伴神社'''（すみよしおおともじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]龍安寺にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|社格]]は旧村社。 == 概要 == [[衣笠山 (京都府)|衣笠山]]などに続く住吉山の山麓、[[京都市道183号衣笠宇多野線|京都市道183号]]（きぬかけの道）の南側にある。南西150mには[[仁和寺]]が、北東150mには[[龍安寺]]がある。[[右京区]]龍安寺や右京区谷口などの[[産土神]]である平凡社 (1997)、p.404。 == 祭神 == * 大伴氏の祖神二神 : [[天忍日命]]（あまのおしひのみこと） : [[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと） * [[住吉三神]] : 上筒男命（うわつつおのみこと） : 中筒男命（なかつつおのみこと） : 底筒男命（そこつつおのみこと） 出典 : 京都古社寺辞典 == 歴史 == ; 伴氏神社 創建年は不明だが、平安京遷都に際して[[大伴氏]]が大和国から京の都に移った際、その氏神として伴氏神社が祀られたものとされる京都市文化観光局文化財保護課 (1994)、p.32。[[続日本後紀]]の天長11年（[[834年]]）の条には伴氏神社の創祀に関する記述があるが、現在の住吉大伴神社とも、[[北野天満宮]]境内にある伴氏社ともいわれている京都市文化観光局文化財保護課 (1994)、p.44。弘仁14年（[[823年]]）に[[淳和天皇]]が即位すると、淳和天皇の諱が大伴だったことから大伴氏は伴氏に改称し、この神社も'''伴氏神社'''（ともうちのじんじゃ、とものうじじんじゃ）と改称された。延喜5年（[[927年]]）にまとめられた[[延喜式神名帳]]にも、[[山城国]][[葛野郡]]上林郷の項に伴氏神社の名が記録されている。貞観6年（[[866年]]）の[[応天門]]の変で[[伴善男]]が失脚し、大伴氏の衰退を機に神社の祭礼も廃絶された槇野ほか (2007)、p.40。 ; 住吉神社 大伴氏の没落後、[[山城国]][[葛野郡]]上林郷の当地は[[左大臣]]・[[藤原実能]]（徳大寺実能）などを輩出した[[徳大寺家]]の領地となった。平安時代末期には徳大寺家によって住吉神が祀られ、'''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）と改称された。中世までの史料では近世の『山城誌』などの史料はこの住吉神社をかつての伴氏神社に比定している。 ; 住吉大伴神社 昭和期に入って再び大伴祖神を祀るべきという意見が強まったため、1942年（昭和17年）には[[天忍日命]]（あまのおしひのみこと）と[[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと）が合祀され、現行の'''住吉大伴神社'''（すみよしおおともじんじゃ）と改称した吉川弘文館編集部 (2010)、p.188。 == 境内 == ; 社殿 現在の本殿は昭和初期に建てられたものであり、間口一間、奥行一間半の縮小型[[住吉造]]で日本唯一の建築である。1978年（昭和53年）には萱葺の本殿屋根が銅葺きに改められた。住吉造による本殿は[[住吉大社]]（大阪市住吉区）、[[住吉神社 (福岡市)|住吉神社]]（福岡市博多区）に見られるのみである。 ; 境内社 往古より十禅師権現社、斎ノ宮社、小松尾明神社が奉斎されていたが、現在は小松尾神社のみが残り、他の末社は小松尾神社に合祀されている。 ; 歌碑 [[大伴家持]]が詠んだ「海ゆかはみつく屍　山ゆかは　草むす屍　大君のへにこそ死なめ　かへりみはせし」の歌碑が境内外苑に保存されている住吉大伴神社境内にある由緒書による。。この歌は[[万葉集]]巻十八にあり、『[[海行かば]]』として[[国民歌]]に選定されている。 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|きぬかけの道から File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|社務所 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|境内社の小松尾神社 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|鳥居と境内 == 交通 == * 最寄駅： [[京福電気鉄道|嵐電]][[京福電気鉄道北野線|北野線]][[妙心寺駅]]または[[龍安寺駅]]から北に400m * 最寄バス停：[[京都市営バス]]バス停「塔ノ下町」または「竜安寺前」から150m == 参考文献 == * 京都市文化観光局文化財保護課『京の古代社寺 -京都の式内社と古代寺院-』（京都市文化財ブックス）京都市文化観光局文化財保護課、1994年 * 槇野修著・山折哲雄監修『京都の寺社505を歩く 下 –洛西・洛北・洛南・洛外編-』（PHP新書）PHP研究所、2007年 * 平凡社『京都・山城寺院神社大事典』平凡社、1997年 * 西田直二郎『洛西花園小史』積善館、1945年 * 吉川弘文館編集部『京都古社寺事典』吉川弘文館、2010年 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[伴林氏神社]] - 大阪府藤井寺市にある神社。同じく道臣命を祀っており、大伴氏の氏族である伴林氏が祭祀に関わっていた。 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしおおともしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:右京区の建築物]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉大伴神社 |画像 = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]龍安寺住吉町1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |祭神 = [[天忍日命]]
[[道臣命]]
[[住吉三神]] |社格 = 旧[[村社]] |創建 = |本殿 = [[住吉造]]檜皮妻入 |別名 = |例祭 =10月16日 |神事 = }} '''住吉大伴神社'''（すみよしおおともじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[右京区]]龍安寺にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|社格]]は旧村社。 == 概要 == [[衣笠山 (京都府)|衣笠山]]などに続く住吉山の山麓、[[京都市道183号衣笠宇多野線|京都市道183号]]（きぬかけの道）の南側にある。南西150mには[[仁和寺]]が、北東150mには[[龍安寺]]がある。[[右京区]]龍安寺や右京区谷口などの[[産土神]]である平凡社 (1997)、p.404。 == 祭神 == * 大伴氏の祖神二神 : [[天忍日命]]（あまのおしひのみこと） : [[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと） * [[住吉三神]] : 上筒男命（うわつつおのみこと） : 中筒男命（なかつつおのみこと） : 底筒男命（そこつつおのみこと） 出典 : 京都古社寺辞典 == 歴史 == ; 伴氏神社 創建年は不明だが、平安京遷都に際して[[大伴氏]]が大和国から京の都に移った際、その氏神として伴氏神社が祀られたものとされる京都市文化観光局文化財保護課 (1994)、p.32。[[続日本後紀]]の天長11年（[[834年]]）の条には伴氏神社の創祀に関する記述があるが、現在の住吉大伴神社とも、[[北野天満宮]]境内にある伴氏社ともいわれている京都市文化観光局文化財保護課 (1994)、p.44。弘仁14年（[[823年]]）に[[淳和天皇]]が即位すると、淳和天皇の諱が大伴だったことから大伴氏は伴氏に改称し、この神社も'''伴氏神社'''（ともうちのじんじゃ、とものうじじんじゃ）と改称された。延喜5年（[[927年]]）にまとめられた[[延喜式神名帳]]にも、[[山城国]][[葛野郡]]上林郷の項に伴氏神社の名が記録されている。貞観6年（[[866年]]）の[[応天門]]の変で[[伴善男]]が失脚し、大伴氏の衰退を機に神社の祭礼も廃絶された槇野ほか (2007)、p.40。 ; 住吉神社 大伴氏の没落後、[[山城国]][[葛野郡]]上林郷の当地は[[左大臣]]・[[藤原実能]]（徳大寺実能）などを輩出した[[徳大寺家]]の領地となった。平安時代末期には徳大寺家によって住吉神が祀られ、'''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）と改称された。中世までの史料では近世の『山城誌』などの史料はこの住吉神社をかつての伴氏神社に比定している。 ; 住吉大伴神社 昭和期に入って再び大伴祖神を祀るべきという意見が強まったため、1942年（昭和17年）には[[天忍日命]]（あまのおしひのみこと）と[[道臣命]]（みちのおみのみこと）が合祀され、現行の'''住吉大伴神社'''（すみよしおおともじんじゃ）と改称した吉川弘文館編集部 (2010)、p.188。 == 境内 == ; 社殿 現在の本殿は昭和初期に建てられたものであり、間口一間、奥行一間半の縮小型[[住吉造]]で日本唯一の建築である。1978年（昭和53年）には萱葺の本殿屋根が銅葺きに改められた。住吉造による本殿は[[住吉大社]]（大阪市住吉区）、[[住吉神社 (福岡市)|住吉神社]]（福岡市博多区）に見られるのみである。 ; 境内社 往古より十禅師権現社、斎ノ宮社、小松尾明神社が奉斎されていたが、現在は小松尾神社のみが残り、他の末社は小松尾神社に合祀されている。 ; 歌碑 [[大伴家持]]が詠んだ「海ゆかはみつく屍　山ゆかは　草むす屍　大君のへにこそ死なめ　かへりみはせし」の歌碑が境内外苑に保存されている住吉大伴神社境内にある由緒書による。。この歌は[[万葉集]]巻十八にあり、『[[海行かば]]』として[[国民歌]]に選定されている。 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|きぬかけの道から File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|社務所 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|境内社の小松尾神社 File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|鳥居と境内 == 交通 == * 最寄駅： [[京福電気鉄道|嵐電]][[京福電気鉄道北野線|北野線]][[妙心寺駅]]または[[龍安寺駅]]から北に400m * 最寄バス停：[[京都市営バス]]バス停「塔ノ下町」または「竜安寺前」から150m == 参考文献 == * 京都市文化観光局文化財保護課『京の古代社寺 -京都の式内社と古代寺院-』（京都市文化財ブックス）京都市文化観光局文化財保護課、1994年 * 槇野修著・山折哲雄監修『京都の寺社505を歩く 下 –洛西・洛北・洛南・洛外編-』（PHP新書）PHP研究所、2007年 * 平凡社『京都・山城寺院神社大事典』平凡社、1997年 * 西田直二郎『洛西花園小史』積善館、1945年 * 吉川弘文館編集部『京都古社寺事典』吉川弘文館、2010年 == 脚注 == === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[伴林氏神社]] - 大阪府藤井寺市にある神社。同じく道臣命を祀っており、大伴氏の氏族である伴林氏が祭祀に関わっていた。 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよしおおともしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:右京区の建築物]] [[Category:右京区の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|住吉大伴神社|version=74941999|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|住吉大伴神社|version=74941999|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = [[File:Sumiyoshi Otomo-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]] |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the [[Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|ja|龍安寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō Ward|ja|右京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Heibonsha (1997), p.404 == Deities Enshrined == * The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命) Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == History == ; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32 An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|ja|続日本後紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44 In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province. After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|ja|応天門の変|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.Makino et al. (2007), p.40 ; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''. ; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188 == Grounds == ; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|ja|住吉造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure, unique in Japan.History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine. In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi Taisha|ja|住吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|ja|住吉神社 (福岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukuoka. ; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it. ; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Volume 18. This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|ja|海行かば|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|ja|京福電気鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at either {{ill|Myōshin-ji Station|ja|妙心寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ryōan-ji Station|ja|龍安寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away == References == * Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Engishiki Shrines and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == See also == * {{ill|Tomobayashi Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan. {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|住吉大伴神社|version=74941999|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:History of Ukyō Ward]] [[Category:Former village shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:null edit to force updates]] [[Category:天忍日命]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[sn:umiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]] [[ja:住吉大伴神社]] [[en:Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine]]
