{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI4.JPG |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Amanōshihi|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = |location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.031667|N|135.717222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Road 183|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都市道183号衣笠宇多野線|1=Kinukake-no-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375135}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|en|Ryōan-ji|WD=Q587371|lt=Ryōan-ji|1=Ryōan-ji}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}.<ref name="Heibonsha404">Heibonsha (1997), p.404</ref>
== Deities Enshrined ==
* The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命)
Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188"/>
== History ==
; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.<ref name="AncientTemples32">Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32</ref> An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}.<ref name="AncientTemples44">Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44</ref>
In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province.<ref name="Heibonsha404"/>
After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|en|Ōtenmon Incident|WD=Q169232|lt=Ōtenmon Incident|1=Ōtenmon Incident}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.<ref name="Heibonsha404"/><ref name="PHP40">Makino et al. (2007), p.40</ref>
; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''.<ref name="PHP40"/><ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188"/>
; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.<ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188">Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188</ref><ref name="Heibonsha404"/>
== Grounds ==
; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} structure, unique in Japan.<ref name="HistoryPlaque">History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine.</ref> In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|WD=Q29682|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)}} in Fukuoka.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/>
; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/>
; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}} Volume 18.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/> This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|en|Umi Yukaba|WD=Q1135905|lt=Umi Yukaba|1=Umi Yukaba}}.
<gallery> File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts </gallery>
== Access ==
* Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|en|Keifuku Electric Railroad|WD=Q3537126|lt=Keifuku Electric Railroad|1=Keifuku Electric Railroad}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Line|1=Kitano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883224}} at either {{ill|Myōshinji Station|en|Myōshinji Station|WD=Q11446899|lt=Myōshin-ji Station|1=Myōshinji Station}} or {{ill|Ryōanji station|en|Ryōanji station|WD=Q11679223|lt=Ryōan-ji Station|1=Ryōanji station}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away
== References ==
* Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Shikinaisha and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|ja|伴林氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomobayashi Uji Shrine|qq=}} - A shrine in Fujidera City, Osaka, also enshrining Michinoomi and related to the Ōtomo clan.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine]][[ja:住吉大伴神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine]][[ja:住吉大伴神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉大伴神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine |image = File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI4.JPG |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}{{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} Ryōanji Sumiyoshi-chō 1 |ISO = JP-26 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 01|緯度秒 = 54 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 2 |ラベル位置 = right |地図 = Japan Kyoto city |deity = {{ill|Amanōshihi|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi}} |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = |本殿 = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} with cypress bark roof, gabled |別名 = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|festival = October 16 |神事 = |location = {{ill|Ukyō Ward|WD=Q580186|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.031667|N|135.717222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine''' (住吉大伴神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}} of {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, near Ryōanji Temple. It holds the status of a former village shrine under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern System of Ranked Shinto Shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Overview ==
The shrine is situated at the foot of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja|住吉山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, south of the {{ill|Kyoto City Road 183|ja|京都市道183号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto City Road 183|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京都市道183号衣笠宇多野線|1=Kinukake-no-michi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375135}} (Kinukake-no-Michi). It is 150 meters southwest of {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}} and 150 meters northeast of {{ill|Ryōan-ji|en|Ryōan-ji|WD=Q587371|lt=Ryōan-ji|1=Ryōan-ji}}. The shrine serves as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} for the local area, including Ryōanji and Taniguchi in {{ill|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q580186|lt=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto|1=Ukyō-ku, Kyoto}}.<ref name="Heibonsha404">Heibonsha (1997), p.404</ref>
== Deities Enshrined ==
* The ancestral deities of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} (あめのおしひのみこと) ** {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} (みちのおみのみこと) * The {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} ** Uwatsutsu-no-mikoto (上筒男命) ** Nakatsutsu-no-mikoto (中筒男命) ** Sokotsutsu-no-mikoto (底筒男命)
Source: {{ill|Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja|京都古社寺辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Ancient Shrines and Temples Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188"/>
== History ==
; Banshi Shrine The year of foundation is unknown, but it is believed to have been established as the ancestral shrine of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} when they moved from Yamato Province to the Heian capital during the relocation of the capital to Heian-kyō.<ref name="AncientTemples32">Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.32</ref> An entry in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}} for the 11th year of Tenchō (834) mentions the founding of Banshi Shrine, which may refer to the present Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine or the Banshi Shrine within the precincts of {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}}.<ref name="AncientTemples44">Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage (1994), p.44</ref>
In the 14th year of Kōnin (823), when Emperor Junna ascended the throne, the Ōtomo clan changed their name to the Banshi clan in honor of the emperor’s personal name. The shrine was then renamed '''Banshi Shrine''' (伴氏神社). The name appears in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Kamikaya-go in Kadono District, Yamashiro Province.<ref name="Heibonsha404"/>
After the Ōtomo clan declined following the {{ill|Ōtenmon Incident|en|Ōtenmon Incident|WD=Q169232|lt=Ōtenmon Incident|1=Ōtenmon Incident}} in Jōgan 6 (866), the shrine's ceremonies fell into disuse.<ref name="Heibonsha404"/><ref name="PHP40">Makino et al. (2007), p.40</ref>
; Sumiyoshi Shrine After the decline of the Ōtomo clan, the area came under the control of the Tokudaiji family, who worshipped the Sumiyoshi deities and renamed the shrine '''Sumiyoshi Shrine'''.<ref name="PHP40"/><ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188"/>
; Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine In the Shōwa era, there was a renewed call to honor the ancestral deities of the Ōtomo clan. In 1942, {{ill|Ame-no-Oshihi|ja|天忍日命|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshihi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Oshihi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシヒ}} and {{ill|Michinoomi|ja|道臣命|1=Michinoomi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|13=User:Immanuelle/Michinoomi|qq=}} were enshrined alongside the Sumiyoshi deities, and the shrine was renamed '''Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine'''.<ref name="AncientShrineDictionary188">Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department (2010), p.188</ref><ref name="Heibonsha404"/>
== Grounds ==
; Shrine Buildings The current main hall, built in the early Shōwa period, is a reduced-scale {{ill|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|en|Sumiyoshi-zukuri|WD=Q3503635|lt=Sumiyoshi-zukuri|1=Sumiyoshi-zukuri}} structure, unique in Japan.<ref name="HistoryPlaque">History plaque at Sumiyoshi Ōtomo Shrine.</ref> In 1978, its cypress bark roof was replaced with copper sheeting. Sumiyoshi-zukuri architecture is otherwise seen only at {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|WD=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} in Osaka and {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|WD=Q29682|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)}} in Fukuoka.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/>
; Other Shrines Originally, the grounds included Tenzen Gongen Shrine, Sai-no-Miya Shrine, and Komatsuo Myōjin Shrine. Today, only Komatsuo Shrine remains, with the others merged into it.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/>
; Monument The precincts house a monument engraved with the poem by {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}: "海ゆかばみつく屍 山ゆかば 草むす屍 大君のへにこそ死なめ かへりみはせし," featured in the {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}} Volume 18.<ref name="HistoryPlaque"/> This poem inspired the Japanese national song {{ill|Umi Yukaba|en|Umi Yukaba|WD=Q1135905|lt=Umi Yukaba|1=Umi Yukaba}}.
<gallery> File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI1.JPG|From Kinukake-no-Michi File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI2.JPG|Shrine office File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI3.JPG|Komatsuo Shrine File:Sumiyoshi Otomo Shrine140606NI5.JPG|Torii and precincts </gallery>
== Access ==
* Nearest station: {{ill|Keifuku Electric Railroad|en|Keifuku Electric Railroad|WD=Q3537126|lt=Keifuku Electric Railroad|1=Keifuku Electric Railroad}}{{ill|Kitano Line|ja|京福電気鉄道北野線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Line|1=Kitano Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883224}} at either {{ill|Myōshinji Station|en|Myōshinji Station|WD=Q11446899|lt=Myōshin-ji Station|1=Myōshinji Station}} or {{ill|Ryōanji station|en|Ryōanji station|WD=Q11679223|lt=Ryōan-ji Station|1=Ryōanji station}}, 400m north * Nearest bus stop: Kyoto City Bus "Tōnoshitachō" or "Ryōanji-mae," 150m away
== References ==
* Kyoto City Bureau of Cultural Heritage, ''Kyoto's Ancient Shrines and Temples - Kyoto's Shikinaisha and Ancient Temples'' (Kyoto City Cultural Heritage Books), 1994. * Makino Osamu, supervised by Yamori Tetsuo, ''505 Kyoto Temples and Shrines to Visit, Vol. 2 - Western, Northern, Southern, and Outer Kyoto'' (PHP Shinsho), PHP Institute, 2007. * Heibonsha, ''Kyoto-Yamashiro Temple and Shrine Encyclopedia'', Heibonsha, 1997. * Nishida Naojirō, ''A Short History of Rakusai Hanazono'', Sekizenkan, 1945. * Yoshikawa Kōbunkan Editorial Department, ''Kyoto Ancient Shrine and Temple Encyclopedia'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2010.
== Notes ==

== See also ==
